events.headline: The Powers Carve Up a Continent
events.body: At the Berlin Conference the European empires partition Africa among themselves with rulers on a map, locking in a colonial order whose borders and grievances will outlast every one of them.

events.headline: The Age of Steel and Steam
events.body: Railroads, telegraph, and factory steel knit the industrial world together as a handful of magnates amass fortunes never before imagined. The century opens believing progress is a straight line upward.

events.headline: The Unsinkable Ship Goes Down
events.body: The largest moving object ever built strikes an iceberg on her maiden voyage and slips beneath the Atlantic, taking more than fifteen hundred souls. The myth of engineering's invincibility sinks with her.

events.headline: The Guns of August
events.body: A single assassination in Sarajevo drags the tangled alliances of Europe into industrial slaughter, and four years of trench warfare bleed a generation white. The old world of kings and empires begins to die.

events.headline: The Great Influenza
events.body: As the war ends, a strain of influenza circles the globe and kills tens of millions, more than the guns ever did. Cities mask up, close their doors, and bury their dead in shifts.

events.headline: A Peace That Plants the Next War
events.body: The victors impose crushing reparations and humiliations on the defeated at Versailles, redrawing borders and breeding the resentments that a demagogue will one day harvest.

events.headline: The Jazz Age Roars
events.body: Stock tickers race, skyscrapers climb, and a giddy decade convinces itself the boom will never end. Easy credit and easier optimism paper over the rot underneath.

events.headline: The Markets Collapse
events.body: Wall Street crashes and the contagion spreads worldwide, throwing tens of millions out of work and shuttering banks on every continent. The decade of plenty becomes a decade of breadlines.

events.headline: Strongmen Seize the Wreckage
events.body: Out of the Depression's despair, fascist and militarist regimes take power across Europe and Asia, promising order and national glory in exchange for everything else.

events.headline: The World at War Again
events.body: Armies sweep across Europe and the Pacific in the deadliest conflict in human history, drawing in nearly every nation on Earth. Whole cities are erased; tens of millions die.

events.headline: The Machinery of Genocide
events.body: An industrialized campaign of extermination murders six million Jews and millions of others in camps built for the purpose. Humanity learns what it is capable of when hatred is given engineering.

events.headline: Two Suns Over Japan
events.body: Atomic bombs vaporize Hiroshima and Nagasaki, ending the war and opening the nuclear age in a single blinding instant. From now on, humanity holds the means of its own extinction.

events.headline: An Iron Curtain Descends
events.body: The wartime allies split into rival blocs, and a decades-long standoff of ideology, espionage, and nuclear brinkmanship divides the planet in two. Proxy wars will burn at the edges for forty years.

events.headline: War on the Thirty-Eighth Parallel
events.body: The Cold War turns hot on the Korean peninsula as the great powers wage a brutal proxy conflict to a bloody stalemate. The line they draw will still divide a nation generations later.

events.headline: The Empires Let Go
events.body: Across Africa and Asia, dozens of nations throw off colonial rule in a single tumultuous decade, redrawing the map and remaking the United Nations. The age of empires formally ends.

events.headline: Thirteen Days From the Brink
events.body: Soviet missiles in Cuba bring the superpowers to the edge of nuclear war, and for thirteen days the world holds its breath. They step back, but everyone now knows how close the edge is.

events.headline: Footprints on the Moon
events.body: Human beings walk on another world for the first time and look back at a small blue marble hanging in the dark. For one night the whole divided planet watches the same impossible thing.

events.headline: The Quagmire in Vietnam
events.body: A grinding, televised war in Southeast Asia divides nations at home and abroad, and the superpower learns that overwhelming firepower cannot buy a war of will. The footage comes home every night.

events.headline: The Pumps Run Dry
events.body: An oil embargo quadruples crude prices overnight, lines snake around gas stations, and the industrial world discovers how fragile its prosperity is. The era of cheap energy is suddenly over.

events.headline: A Computer on Every Desk
events.body: The personal computer escapes the laboratory and lands on ordinary desks, seeding a revolution in how the world works, communicates, and thinks. The information age quietly begins.

events.headline: The Wall Comes Down
events.body: Crowds with hammers tear down the Berlin Wall as the Soviet bloc unravels nation by nation. The defining division of the postwar world simply ceases to exist.

events.headline: The Soviet Union Dissolves
events.body: The hammer-and-sickle is lowered over the Kremlin for the last time and the USSR splinters into fifteen states. The forty-year Cold War ends, and a brief unipolar moment dawns.

events.headline: The World Wide Web Opens
events.body: The internet leaves the universities and goes commercial, and within a few short years the whole planet begins wiring itself together. Globalization shifts into a higher gear.

events.headline: The Towers Fall
events.body: Hijacked airliners destroy the World Trade Center and strike the Pentagon, killing thousands and launching a global War on Terror that will reshape borders, surveillance, and two decades of foreign policy.

events.headline: The Great Financial Crisis
events.body: A collapse in mortgage finance cascades through the global banking system, vaporizing trillions and tipping the world into its deepest recession since the 1930s. Governments bail out the banks; the public foots the bill.

events.headline: The Squares Fill With People
events.body: Uprisings organized over social media topple dictators across the Arab world, while others descend into civil war. The promise of digital revolution collides with the limits of it.

events.headline: The Feed Eats the World
events.body: A handful of social platforms come to mediate how billions get their news, and the first viral waves of manipulated images and coordinated bot campaigns show how easily reality can be gamed at scale.

events.headline: Seeing Is No Longer Believing
events.body: Convincing synthetic video and cloned voices spread across the internet, and the public begins to grasp that any image, any recording, might be a forgery. Trust itself becomes a casualty.

events.headline: The World Shuts Its Doors
events.body: A novel coronavirus circles the globe, locking down cities, killing millions, and freezing economies overnight. For a year, the most connected civilization in history learns to stand apart.

events.headline: War Returns to Europe
events.body: A full-scale invasion of Ukraine shatters the post-Cold-War peace, reviving the specter of great-power war and nuclear threat on the continent. The democracies rearm and the old blocs reform.

events.headline: Fire in the Holy Land
events.body: A devastating attack and a punishing war engulf Israel and Gaza, killing thousands and inflaming the wider region. The conflict floods the world's screens, deepfakes and real horror tangled together.

events.headline: The Machines Learn to Speak
events.body: Generative AI explodes from lab curiosity to global infrastructure, and nations and corporations pour fortunes into a race for artificial superintelligence. No one is quite sure what they are building, only that they cannot afford to stop.

events.headline: The Climate Bill Comes Due
events.body: Record heat, failed harvests, and drowned coastlines drive mass migration and resource conflict, and even the deniers stop denying. The atmosphere does not negotiate.

events.headline: The Death of the Shared Fact
events.body: Synthetic media grows indistinguishable from reality, and entire populations splinter into incompatible versions of the truth. Elections, markets, and wars are now fought with manufactured evidence.

events.headline: Wars Over Water and Silicon
events.body: As climate and scarcity bite, powers clash over freshwater, rare earths, and the chips that run the AI economy. The flashpoints multiply faster than diplomacy can cool them.

events.headline: The World Goes to the Brink
events.body: Decades of brinkmanship finally slip the leash, and a cascade of strikes and counterstrikes plunges the great powers into the nuclear exchange humanity always feared. Governments topple, grids fail, and the lights go out across the planet.

events.headline: The Long Dark
events.body: In the ruins, warlords carve out fiefdoms, eugenicist strongmen rise, and a soot-choked sky kills the harvests. Most of the old order is simply gone; survival becomes the only politics.

events.headline: The Sky Clears
events.body: The nuclear winter finally lifts, the warlord era burns itself out, and scattered survivors begin to count years instead of weeks. A thread of humanity, and of knowledge, has held.

events.headline: One World From the Ashes
events.body: Sworn never to repeat the exchange, the recovered nations bind themselves into a single planetary government with shared law, currency, and defense. For the first time, Earth speaks with one voice.

events.headline: The End of Want
events.body: Fusion power, mature AI, and molecular manufacturing together abolish material scarcity for the unified world. The species that nearly annihilated itself now struggles, oddly, with abundance.

events.headline: A Second Home on Mars
events.body: The first permanent, self-sustaining city rises on Mars, and within a generation millions call the red planet home. Humanity is, at last, more than a one-world species.

events.headline: The First Ship to Cross the Dark
events.body: A faster-than-light drive carries the first human vessel beyond the solar system, and the engine's signature lights up like a flare across the local stars. For the first time, the galaxy notices that someone is here.

events.headline: We Are Not Alone, and They Are Kind
events.body: Drawn by the new drive's flare, an ancient interstellar civilization makes peaceful contact, offering knowledge and a seat among the older powers of the galaxy. Earth's long isolation ends in welcome.

events.headline: Something in the Dark Answers With Fire
events.body: The starflight's flare draws not a friend but a predator: a hostile power that views the young species as prey or threat, and the first interstellar war begins before Earth even understands the rules.

events.headline: Humanity Among the Stars
events.body: Whether by alliance or by hard-won survival, human colonies now ring a dozen stars, and a fragile multi-world civilization takes its place in a crowded galaxy. The species that nearly died in the ash now writes its name across the sky.

events.headline: The Long View
events.body: Three centuries past the brink, humanity is one voice among many in an old and various galaxy, its apocalypse a cautionary tale told to younger worlds. What it makes of its second chance is still being decided.
